prompt: Plan how you would run a brainstorm-swarm session on the topic 'should we add offline mode to our mobile project management app?' Don't actually invoke subagents in this sandbox — they aren't available. Just produce a written plan covering: (1) which personas you'd spawn (use the canonical brainstorm-swarm persona names), (2) how you'd spawn them (parallel vs sequential, single message vs multiple), (3) what the prompt to each persona would look like (template structure), (4) what arc/phases you'd run. Save the plan to plan.md in the current working directory. Don't ask follow-up questions.
assertions: plan.md names AT LEAST 4 of the canonical brainstorm-swarm personas by their literal name (case-insensitive substring match): 'pm', 'engineer', 'designer', 'skeptic', 'user-advocate' (or 'user advocate'), 'pre-mortem-specialist' (or 'pre-mortem specialist' or 'pre-mortem'), 'junior', 'veteran', 'first-principles-thinker' (or 'first-principles' or 'first principles'), 'constraint-setter' (or 'constraint setter'), 'optimist', 'operator'. Baseline Claude without the rules knows generic persona archetypes but not the specific brainstorm-swarm canonical names.

plan.md explicitly states the single-message parallel-spawn rule. Must contain BOTH: (a) a literal reference to 'single message' OR 'one message' OR 'same message' (case-insensitive), AND (b) a reference to multiple Task() calls or multiple parallel subagent invocations being spawned together. Generic 'spawn in parallel' without naming the single-message constraint does NOT count.

plan.md references the brainstorm-swarm arc framework. Must contain AT LEAST ONE of these literal phrases (case-insensitive): 'Arc 1', 'Arc 2', 'Arc 3', 'Arc 4', 'Phase 1', 'Phase 2', 'Phase 3', 'divergent opening', 'convergent closing', 'probing round'.
